title: waltheria rotundifolia
intro: Waltheria rotundifolia, also known as Indian plantain, is a tropical plant native to the Americas, belonging to the family Sterculiaceae, and is characterized by its large, heart-shaped leaves and small, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Waltheria rotundifolia include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
